name: Mahbubul Alam Majumdar, PhD
biography:
I am a mathematician with a wide array of interests. My interests in computer science are development oriented -- how can we use computer science techniques and computational power to make the lives of Bangladeshis better?  My academic interests are in string theory, early universe cosmology, quantum computing and machine learning. 

For those wanting to work with me or do a thesis, an outline of my interests is below.

Computer Science

Machine Learning
Computational Finance
Cryptocurrencies, Blockchain and related ideas
Quantum Computing, Quantum Algorithms and Cryptography
Theoretical Physics

String cosmology
Black Hole Information Paradox and Quantum Information Theory
Singularity resolution
Resurgence and non-perturbative phenomena
Mathematics

Elliptic curves, Modular forms and Moonshine
Other

Game Theory, Auctions and applications of game theory to social sciences
Sampling techniques and polling
One of my primary goals is the guiding of  the best mathematical and scientific talent in Bangladesh and the creation of a community of extremely capable Bangladeshi mathematicians, theoretical physicists and computer scientists. I am MIT's Regional Coordinator and the National Coach of the Bangladesh Mathematical Olympiad Team. I try to contribute to nation-building by injecting technical sophistication into the business and financial spaces and supporting entrepreneurs by finding them financial support and providing technical expertise to turn their ideas into successful companies to create a modern Bangladesh. 

I have taught a wide array of courses ranging from Machine Learning to Number Theory to Quantum Field Theory.

education: CSE Department 4th floor, Room No # 4G32, Brac University, Kha 224 Bir Uttam Rafiqul Islam Avenue, Merul Badda, Dhaka, Bangladesh

Dr. Sadia Hamid Kazi is an Associate Professor in the Computer Science and Engineering Department under the School of Data and Sciences at Brac University. She received her Ph.D. degree in Information Systems with an emphasis on cognitive modeling. She has been with Brac University since 2003. Prior to joining BRAC University, she had five years of industry experience. Her research interests lie in the broad areas of Adhoc networking, network security, and cognitive modeling. She was a part of a Malaysian Government initiative under the Exploratory Research Grant Scheme (ERGS), under which she has a patent from the Malaysian IPO authority.

name: Dr. Mohammad Kaykobad
biography:
Professor Kaykobad served as a Distinguished Professor of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. He retired from CSE Department, BUET in 2020.  He was awarded the Gold Medal for contribution to ICT Education by the Bangladesh Computer Society in 2005. In 2006 he won the Bangladesh Academy of Sciences Gold Medal for Physical Sciences Senior Group. He was one of the  pioneers in introducing competitive programming in Bangladesh. He led the BUET team to the ACM Finals numerous times and his 2000 team finished 11th in the world. He received the Outstanding Coach Award at the ACM ICPC Finals held in Honolulu, Hawaii in 2002. In 2013 he was awarded the Senior Coach Award at the ACM ICPC Finals in St. Petersburg. In 2019 he was awarded ICPC Foundation Life Coach Award at Porto, Portugal. He is one of the pioneers in introducing Mathematics, Informatics and Science   Olympiads   in Bangladesh. He has worked as a Director of the Dhaka and Chittagong Stock Exchanges for many years and is a member of the Academic Councils and Syndicates of several universities. He is currently an Independent Director of Sonali Bank. 

Dr. Kaykobad was a Visiting Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, the Chinese University of Hong Kong during the years 2000 and 2001, KAIST, Korea, Monash University Gippsland Campus, Australian National University, Amritapuri University, India Kyong Hee University, Korea. He is keen to popularize science and develop analytical skill in Bangladeshi students. He is a very frequent column writer inspiring educational excellence and patriotism in young people. Dr Kaykobad is a Fellow of Bangladesh Academy of Sciences.

Worked as coordinator from Bangladesh in the EU sponsored (EM ECW Ref: 149674-EM-1-2008-1-UK-ERAMUNDUS) eLINK ( e ast west L ink for I nnovation, N etworking and K nowledge exchange) project. In this project five host EU institutions invited third country students, teachers and researchers to carry out 10 months study/research activities in their premises. These institutions are University of Bradford, UK, Staffordshire University, UK, Corvinus University of Budapest, Hungary, University of Sannio, Italy and University LUMIERE LYON2, France. The Asian partners who took part in the project are United International University, Bangladesh, College of Science and Technology, Bhutan, Kantipur Engineering College, Nepal, Mohammed Ali Jinnah University, Pakistan, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, China and Chiang Mai University, Thailand. From a total of 334 scholarships worth of 5.5 million Euro, Bangladesh enjoyed 85 scholarships under undergraduate, masters, ph.d, post doctoral and teachers training categories.

Worked as joint coordinator in the EU sponsored Erasmus Mundus Action 2 partnerships (372242-1-2012-1-UK-ERA MUNDUS –EMA 21) Clink ( Centre of Excellence for Learning, Innovation, Networking and Knowledge) project. In this project six host EU institutions invited third country students, teachers and researchers to carry out 10/20 months study/research activities in their premises. These institutions are University of Northumbria at Newcastle, UK, Staffordshire University, UK, Corvinus University of Budapest, Hungary, University of Bremen, Germany and University LUMIERE LYON2, France and Gheorghe Asachi Technical University of Lasi, Romania. The Asian partners who took part in the project are United International University, Bangladesh, Royal University, Bhutan, Kantipur Engineering College, Nepal, Mohammed Ali Jinnah University, Pakistan, Jadavpur University, India, Northeastern University, China and Chiang Mai University, Thailand and University Putra, Malaysia. From a total of 112 scholarships, Bangladesh enjoyed 22 scholarships under undergraduate, masters, ph.d, post doctoral and teachers training categories.

Worked as joint coordinator in the EU (ERUSMUS MUNDUS) sponsored FUSION (Featured eUrope and South asIa mObility Network) project which aims to foster partnerships of emerging Asian countries (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal, Pakistan, China, India, and Thailand) with the EU countries (Bulgaria, France Germany, Hungary, Italy, Portugal, Sweden, and UK) to reinforce the existing collaborations developed through the EU funded projects. In this project ten host EU institutions invite third country students, teachers and researchers to carry out 10/20 months study/research activities in their premises. Similarly EU students and researchers will carry out 6/10 months study/research in Asian universities. All the cost involved will be borne by EU. The EU partners are Bournemouth University , United Kingdom, Rusenski universitet “Angel Kanchev”, Bulgaria, Université LUMIERE LYON2 , France, University of Bremen , Germany, Corvinus University of Budapest , Hungary, University of Sannio, Italy, Universidade de Évora, Portugal, Mälardalen University, Sweden and University of Northumbria at Newcastle , United Kingdom. The Asian partners are Information and Communication Technology Institute, Afghanistan, Kabul University, Afghanistan, United International University , Bangladesh, College of Science and Technology, Royal University of Bhutan , Bhutan, Chengdu University, China, Zhongyuan University of Technology, China, Asia Pacific Institute of Information Technology SD India, India, Gulbarga University , India, Kantipur Engineering College , Nepal, Mohammed Ali Jinnah University , Islamabad, Pakistan and Chiang Mai University , Thailand. This 48 months project is worth of 3.05 million Euro and is aimed to offer 151 scholarships in total.

Worked as coordinator from Bangladesh in the EU sponsored (EM ECW Ref: 149674-EM-1-2008-1-UK-ERAMUNDUS) eLINK ( e ast west L ink for I nnovation, N etworking and K nowledge exchange) project. In this project five host EU institutions invited third country students, teachers and researchers to carry out 10 months study/research activities in their premises. These institutions are University of Bradford, UK, Staffordshire University, UK, Corvinus University of Budapest, Hungary, University of Sannio, Italy and University LUMIERE LYON2, France. The Asian partners who took part in the project are United International University, Bangladesh, College of Science and Technology, Bhutan, Kantipur Engineering College, Nepal, Mohammed Ali Jinnah University, Pakistan, Shanghai Jiao Tong University, China and Chiang Mai University, Thailand. From a total of 334 scholarships worth of 5.5 million Euro, Bangladesh enjoyed 85 scholarships under undergraduate, masters, ph.d, post doctoral and teachers training categories.

Worked as joint coordinator in the EU sponsored Erasmus Mundus Action 2 partnerships (372242-1-2012-1-UK-ERA MUNDUS –EMA 21) Clink ( Centre of Excellence for Learning, Innovation, Networking and Knowledge) project. In this project six host EU institutions invited third country students, teachers and researchers to carry out 10/20 months study/research activities in their premises. These institutions are University of Northumbria at Newcastle, UK, Staffordshire University, UK, Corvinus University of Budapest, Hungary, University of Bremen, Germany and University LUMIERE LYON2, France and Gheorghe Asachi Technical University of Lasi, Romania. The Asian partners who took part in the project are United International University, Bangladesh, Royal University, Bhutan, Kantipur Engineering College, Nepal, Mohammed Ali Jinnah University, Pakistan, Jadavpur University, India, Northeastern University, China and Chiang Mai University, Thailand and University Putra, Malaysia. From a total of 112 scholarships, Bangladesh enjoyed 22 scholarships under undergraduate, masters, ph.d, post doctoral and teachers training categories.

Worked as joint coordinator in the EU (ERUSMUS MUNDUS) sponsored FUSION (Featured eUrope and South asIa mObility Network) project which aims to foster partnerships of emerging Asian countries (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal, Pakistan, China, India, and Thailand) with the EU countries (Bulgaria, France Germany, Hungary, Italy, Portugal, Sweden, and UK) to reinforce the existing collaborations developed through the EU funded projects. In this project ten host EU institutions invite third country students, teachers and researchers to carry out 10/20 months study/research activities in their premises. Similarly EU students and researchers will carry out 6/10 months study/research in Asian universities. All the cost involved will be borne by EU. The EU partners are Bournemouth University , United Kingdom, Rusenski universitet “Angel Kanchev”, Bulgaria, Université LUMIERE LYON2 , France, University of Bremen , Germany, Corvinus University of Budapest , Hungary, University of Sannio, Italy, Universidade de Évora, Portugal, Mälardalen University, Sweden and University of Northumbria at Newcastle , United Kingdom. The Asian partners are Information and Communication Technology Institute, Afghanistan, Kabul University, Afghanistan, United International University , Bangladesh, College of Science and Technology, Royal University of Bhutan , Bhutan, Chengdu University, China, Zhongyuan University of Technology, China, Asia Pacific Institute of Information Technology SD India, India, Gulbarga University , India, Kantipur Engineering College , Nepal, Mohammed Ali Jinnah University , Islamabad, Pakistan and Chiang Mai University , Thailand. This 48 months project is worth of 3.05 million Euro and is aimed to offer 151 scholarships in total.

biography: Matin S. Abdullah received M.Sc. degree in Computer Science in 2002 from University of Houston, USA. He received B.Sc. degree in Electrical Engineering from the same University. He is also currently an Assistant Professor in Computer Science and Engineering department and Associated Director of Center for Research on Bangla Language Processing, BRAC University, Bangladesh. From 2005 to 2006 he served as Lead System Architect, Grameen Software Ltd., Bangladesh the flagship technology outfit of Grameen family of organizations founded by Nobel Peace Prize winner Prof. Muhammad Yunus. Before that he worked as a Software developer for BMC Software, USA.

name: Dr. Md. Khalilur Rahman
biography: Dr. Md. Khalilur Rahman is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University. He was the principal investigator of Nation’s first Nano-satellite program which is named as “BRAC ONNESHA” by BRAC University. He was assigned as the Acting Chairman of CSE department from 1st Sep-14 to 31st Dec-2014. He is the founder of Nano-Satellite Technology Research (NASTER) Lab, BRAC University Robotics lab and the founder advisor of BRAC University Robotics club. The robot named CHONDROBOT, MONGOL-TORI and BRACU-DUBURI was designed and built under his peer supervision. Different versions of these Robots are regularly attending in different international competitions such as NASA Lunabotic Mining Competition USA, University Rover Challenge USA, Singapore Autonomous Underwater Vehicle Competition etc. Dr. Khalil has joined in BRAC University on 2009 immediately after completing his PhD degree from Kyushu Institute of Technology, Japan and promoted as associate professor on 2013. He was a MONBUKAGAKUSHO scholar and his PhD Research topic was Natural Language Processing. But now his research is diverted into Space Science & Engineering, Robotics and Embedded Systems. He was born on 1976 in a river bank city Sirajganj of Bangladesh. After completing his Secondary and Higher Secondary Schooling he took admission in Institute of Science and Technology under National University for graduation and post-graduation degrees. Dr. Khalil became point of contact (POC) of UNISEC-Global (University Space Engineering Consortium) on 2014. In his research and work he could build a very good relationship with government organizations like BTRC, SPARRSO, Atomic Energy Commission, CARGS, ICT Ministry etc. He is also a technical committee member of a Number of International conferences and Journals. He is an IEEE member. S21KT is his amateur radio call sign

professionalActivity: Founder of Robotics Lab of Brac University under which tens of robotics and industrial automation researches are being conducted. Followings are few signature R&D under this Lab

name: Dr. Md. Golam Rabiul Alam
biography: Md. Golam Rabiul Alam is a Professor at the Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University. He received Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Kyung Hee University, South Korea in 2017. He served as a Post-doctoral researcher in Computer Science and Engineering Department, at Kyung Hee University, Korea from March 2017 to February 2018. Dr. Rabiul Alam received B.S. and M.S. degrees in Computer Science and Engineering, and Information Technology from Khulna University and University of Dhaka respectively.   Dr. Rabiul Alam has published around 170 research articles in reputed journals, and conference proceedings. He has also 3 registered patents on ambient assisted living, mobile cloud computing, and mobile fog computing, respectively. Dr. Rabiul Alam's research interests include Mental-health informatics, Ambient intelligence systems, Mobile-cloud computing, Edge computing, Affective computing, and UAV Image processing.  He is a member of the IEEE Computer Society and received several best paper awards at national and international research conferences.
researchInterests: Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Reinforcement Learning
Health informatics, Edge computing, and Medical Image Processing
Affective Computing, Mental Health, and Well-being

name: Dr. Md Sadek Ferdous
biography:
Dr. Ferdous is a Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering of the BRAC University, Dhaka, Bangladesh. He is also an Honorary Research Fellow at the Institute for Security Science and Technology of Imperial College London. 

 

Previously, he was an ERCIM (European Research Consortium for Informatics and Mathematics) Alain Bensoussan Research Fellow at Fraunhofer Institute for Applied Information Technology (FIT), Sankt Augustin, Germany. During this one-year research fellowship he researched on Self-sovereign Identity Management using Blockchain Technology. He was supervised by Prof. Wolfgang Prinz. 

 

Previously, he was an Assistant Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering of the Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST), Sylhet Bangladesh. During my SUST tenure, I established the first-ever blockchain-focused security research laboratory, called SUBSEL (Shahjalal University Blockchain and SEcurity Laboratory), in Bangladesh at SUST. Before joining SUST I worked as a Research Associate at the Centre for Financial Technology of the Imperial College Business School, Imperial College London, UK. I was involved in a project in collaboration with Citi Group to explore how the blockchain technology could be leveraged to improve email security for any global financial institution. I am still associated with the Centre to continue this research remotely from Bangladesh. 

 

While at Imperial, Dr. Ferdous had the opportunity to work at the UK Cabinet Office as their first Technology Fellow. He was a part of a joint-project involving the UK Cabinet Office, UK Ministry of Justice and UK Police in which I explored how the blockchain technology could be leveraged for securing digital evidences. He designed and developed a proof of concept which the UK ministry of Justice exploring the possibility for real-life trial. This has been a very successful project and it received world-wide media attention. Please check the Media link to view a few media coverages. He worked as a Research Fellow at the Electronics and Computer Science of the University of Southampton in a Horizon 2020 cloud security project called SUNFISH in between September, 2015 to June, 2017. In this project, it was investigated how the blockchain technology could be exploited to increase the security in a cloud federation. He was a Post-doctoral Research Assistant at the School of Computing Science of the University of Glasgow in a project called the Integrated Multimedia City Data (iMCD). This was part of an ESRC funded Urban Big Data Centre, Glasgow under the umbrella of Big Data Theme. He was responsible for conducting research and developing systems for collating a large collection of textual and multimedia data from online news sources, microblogs, social sharing sites such as Youtube & flickr and wearable sensors such as life-loggers and GPS devices and then to analyse this data set to make sense. 

 

Dr. Ferdous was a SICSA (Scottish Informatics and Computer Science Alliance) Funded, PhD student at the University of Glasgow. My thesis title is User-controlled Identity Management Systems using Mobile Devices in which I investigated how modern smartphones, ever increasing in power and size, could be used for managing our online identities. My primary supervisor was Dr. Ron Poet and secondary supervisor was Dr. Gethin Norman.

 

Dr. Ferdous actively advise the Government of Bangladesh and different other Bangladeshi organisations in different capacities, particularly in Blockchain. He is the principal author of the National Blockchain Strategy document of Bangladesh.

name: Dr. Amitabha Chakrabarty
biography:
Professor Chakrabarty currently holds the position of a Computer Science and Engineering Professor at Brac University. Previously, he worked at the Independent University, Bangladesh (IUB), overseeing IT infrastructures. His educational journey includes a Ph.D. earned from Dublin City University (DCU) in 2012, complemented by B.Sc. and M.Sc. degrees from the University of Rajshahi's Department of Computer Science and Engineering. Additionally, he pursued a second M.Sc. in Telecommunication Engineering at Independent University, Bangladesh.

His research interests span diverse areas such as the Internet of Things (IoT), Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Computer Networking, Embedded Systems, and Switching Theory. With a prolific publication record of over 100 research papers across national and international journals and conferences, Professor Chakrabarty contributes significantly to his field.

Beyond academia, he actively participates in various project consultations in development sectors. He also serves as a jury member in national IT competitions and serves as a reviewer for various international conferences and journals.

professionalActivity: While participating in the "International Faculty Exchange Program (IFEP)" at Chandigarh University, I taught undergraduate, postgraduate, and Ph.D. students. I also had the opportunity to engage in open discussions with faculty members regarding current research topics in computer science and shared my insights on research approaches. Furthermore, I actively participated in strategic meetings where I provided input toward the design of the Bachelor of Computer Application curriculum. It is worth noting that Chandigarh University made an impressive debut in the 2023 QS World Rankings, securing a rank of 801-1000 and becoming the youngest university in the world to achieve such a feat.

As part of the Erasmus+ ICM program, I participated in a faculty exchange program at Middle East Technical University (METU) in Ankara, Turkey. METU is globally recognized as one of the top universities in Europe. I was the sole candidate selected from Bangladesh and spent one week as a visiting faculty member in the Computer Engineering School. During my time at METU, I had the opportunity to present my research to both undergraduate students and faculty members.

Professor Chakrabarty currently holds the position of a Computer Science and Engineering Professor at Brac University. Previously, he worked at the Independent University, Bangladesh (IUB), overseeing IT infrastructures. His educational journey includes a Ph.D. earned from Dublin City University (DCU) in 2012, complemented by B.Sc. and M.Sc. degrees from the University of Rajshahi's Department of Computer Science and Engineering. Additionally, he pursued a second M.Sc. in Telecommunication Engineering at Independent University, Bangladesh.

During my time at METU, I had the opportunity to present my research to both undergraduate students and faculty members.

name: Dr. Farida Chowdhury
biography:
Dr. Farida Chowdhury is a Professor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, BRAC University since July 2023. Previously she was a Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at Shahjalal University of Science and Technology (SUST), Sylhet.

 

She completed her PhD in Computing Science from the University of Stirling, UK. Her PhD thesis title was “Structured Peer-to-Peer Overlays for NATed Churn Intensive Networks”. She completed her MSc. in Network and e-Business Centred Computing which was an Erasmus Mundus Joint Master's Programme between the University of Reading, UK; Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, Greece and Universidad Carlos(III) de Madrid, Spain.

 

She is one of the authors of a book titled Digital Technology (ডিজিটাল প্রযুক্তি) of Class VI of National Curriculum and Textbook Board (NCTB), Bangladesh. She was a visiting research scientist at the Fraunhofer Institute for Applied Information Technology (Fraunhofer FIT), Germany from May, 2022 to August, 2023. During her SUST tenure, she also worked as the Coordinator of Pipilika, the first Bengali search engine, in between August, 2016 to December, 2019. 

 

Her current research interests include Human Computer Interaction (HCI), Security Usability, Privacy and Networking. She published numerous research articles in reputed international academic journals, conferences and workshops.

name: Dr. Swakkhar Shatabda
biography:
Professor Shatabda earned his Ph.D. from Griffith University in 2014, focusing on AI algorithms for protein structure prediction. He has published widely in reputed journals and conferences, served on editorial boards and review panels, and contributed to the Board of Accreditation for Engineering and Technical Education (BAETE), IEB. His international research includes a stint in BioGeM Institute, Italy. He continues to supervise research projects and mentor students, having overseen over thirty undergraduate projects and more than twenty master’s theses. Additionally, he has authored educational books for children on computing.

 

From 2014 to 2021, he was the Undergraduate Program Coordinator at United International University (UIU), where he significantly contributed to curriculum development and faculty mentoring. He played a key role in securing UIU’s Computer Science and Engineering department's accreditations in 2015 and 2019. Additionally, he led the Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) at UIU from 2022 to 2023 and founded Bangladesh’s first undergraduate Data Science program.

Professor Shatabda earned his Ph.D. from Griffith University in 2014, focusing on AI algorithms for protein structure prediction. He has published widely in reputed journals and conferences, served on editorial boards and review panels, and contributed to the Board of Accreditation for Engineering and Technical Education (BAETE), IEB. His international research includes a stint in BioGeM Institute, Italy. He continues to supervise research projects and mentor students, having overseen over thirty undergraduate projects and more than twenty master’s theses. Additionally, he has authored educational books for children on computing.

From 2014 to 2021, he was the Undergraduate Program Coordinator at United International University (UIU), where he significantly contributed to curriculum development and faculty mentoring. He played a key role in securing UIU’s Computer Science and Engineering department's accreditations in 2015 and 2019. Additionally, he led the Institutional Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC) at UIU from 2022 to 2023 and founded Bangladesh’s first undergraduate Data Science program.

name: Abu Mohammad Khan
biography: Abu Mohammad Khan completed his school and college from Dhaka Collegiate School and Notre Dame college respectively. He finished both his B.Sc. with Honors and M.Sc. with thesis from the University of Dhaka. He Earned the Diploma in High Energy Physics from the Abdus Salam International Centre for Theoretical Physics, Trieste, Italy. Then he went the University of Cambridge, UK, to study Part III of the Mathematical Tripos (equivalent to a Master’s degree) in Applied Mathematics and Theoretical Physics at DAMTP. Afterwards, he moved to the Department of Physics, University of Florida, USA, for graduate study and earned the PhD degree in Mathematical Physics.

name: Dr. Muhammad Nur Yanhaona
biography: Muhammad Nur Yanhaona completed his bachelor's in Computer Science & Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering & Technology (BUET) and got his master's and Ph.D. degrees from the University of Virginia, USA. He worked in the software industry for a decade in various roles, including software development, system architecture design, team leading, and design consultancy. He resigned from the software industry as a distinguished software architect and joined BRAC as a faculty member in 2021. His research and development work spans multiple domains, including blockchain technology, application security, databases, wide-area distributed systems, graph theory, and high-performance computing & programming languages.

awards: Dr. Md. Ashraful Alam is working as an Associate Professor and the Director of CVIS Research Lab (Computer Vision and Intelligent Systems) at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering in BRAC University. Prior to joining BRAC University, Dr. Alam was working as a Post-Doc. at the School of Information and Communication Engineering in Chungbuk National University, South Korea. He received his Doctor of Engineering degree in Computer and Communication Engineering from the same university in February 2015. During his PhD program, he had been awarded “BK21 Best Student Award” in 2014 for his outstanding achievements as a BK21 scholar. Dr. Alam has published more than 100 papers in reputed international journals and conference proceedings. Dr. Alam's research interests include computer vision, image processing, machine learning, deep learning, machine vision, 3-D vision, medical data and image analytics, virtual and augmented reality. He is also engaged as Reviewer, Editorial Board Member, and Lead Guest Editor (Special issue) in reputed international journals.
2) BK21 Scholarship (2010/03- 2015/02): Fully Funded PhD Scholarship funded by the Korean Government for pursuing the PhD program at Chungbuk National University, South Korea.

Dr. Muhammad Iqbal Hossain is an Associate Professor of the Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University. Significant achievements and rigorous pursuits mark his academic journey. Dr. Hossain earned his Ph.D. in Computer Engineering from Kyungpook National University, South Korea, in January 2018. During his doctoral tenure, he actively participated in the Embedded Software Engineering Lab. His contributions to this lab resulted in a substantial collection of peer-reviewed journals and presentations at both international and domestic conferences in Korea. Dr. Hossain's commitment to staying at the forefront of his field is evident through his active participation in various international conferences and symposiums, which have enriched his perspectives and insights.

Dr. Hossain's scholarly pursuits have garnered him recognition beyond his academic accomplishments. His expertise has led him to assume significant roles as a Reviewer and Editorial Board Member for reputable international journals and conferences. Moreover, his remarkable academic journey was facilitated by prestigious scholarships, including the Korean Government Scholarship (GKS) in 2009, which enabled his graduate studies. Subsequently, his exceptional dedication and contributions were acknowledged with the KNU Honors Scholarship during his doctoral pursuit.

Dr. Iqbal’s research interests primarily focus on Information security (Including Cryptography, Access control, and privacy), AI and Machine learning, Model-based Software Engineering, Software Testing and Verification, and automation.

name: Dr. Farig Yousuf Sadeque
biography:
r. Farig Sadeque is an Associate Professor of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. Before joining BRAC University, Dr. Sadeque was an Associate Research Scientist at the Educational Testing Service (ETS), the largest private non-profit educational testing organization in the world. He also worked as a postdoctoral research fellow at Harvard University where he was jointly appointed by the Harvard Medical School and Boston Children’s Hospital, and was a part of the Computational Health Informatics Program (CHIP) Laboratory.

 

Dr. Sadeque completed his PhD in Information Science with a minor in Computer Science from The University of Arizona under the supervision of Dr. Steven Bethard. He completed his undergrad from Bangladesh University of Engineering in Technology, with a major in Computer Science and Engineering. His research interests include Computational Linguistics and Applied Natural Language Processing, Machine Learning, Computational Social Science, Psycholinguisitcs and Sociolinguistics.

name: Dr. Md. Sakir Hossain
biography:
Dr. Md. Sakir Hossain is currently serving as an Associate Professor in the Department of Computer Science at BRAC University, Bangladesh. Prior to this, he held the same position in the Department of Computer Science at American International University-Bangladesh (AIUB).

Dr. Hossain is an accomplished academic and researcher with expertise in communications, cybersecurity, and signal processing. He earned his Ph.D. in the field of Wireless Networks from Saitama University, Japan, and received both his B.Sc. and M.Sc. degrees in Information and Communication Engineering from the University of Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He also conducted postdoctoral research at the Czech Technical University in Prague, Czech Republic.

His research interests cover a broad range of topics, including applied machine learning, cybersecurity, medical signal processing, and wireless networks.

Dr. Hossain is a Senior Member of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), USA.

name: Dr. Aniqua Nusrat Zereen
biography: Aniqua Zereen completed her PhD in Computer Science from Asian Institute of Technology, Thailand, in 2023. She worked as a Research Assistant at the Artificial Intelligence Center at AIT for over 2 years and successfully completed a national project for 'NBTC' in Thailand. She also worked as a co-investigator for a project at Mahidol University, Thailand. Aniqua is a recipient of the Bangabandhu Science and Technology Fellowship Trust. Before joining AIT as a PhD student, she served as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University from 2013 to 2017. Additionally, she worked as a summer student at CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research, in 2013.

name: Mr. Annajiat Alim Rasel
biography: Annajiat Alim Rasel received M.Sc. degree in Computer Science and Engineering in 2019 from BRAC University, Bangladesh. He is currently serving as a Sr. Lecturer and Undergraduate Coordinator of Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) under School of Data and Sciences (SDS) at BRAC University. He oversees the BRAC University Computer Club (BUCC) and IEEE Computer Society Student Branch Chapter as the faculty advisor. Prior to joining as a faculty member of the CSE department in 2009, he has served as a Lab Tutor (aka Teaching Assistant) in the same department. Before that, he was a research intern at Center for Research on Bangla Language Processing (CRBLP) lab at BRAC University. He participated in ACM ICPC related national and international programming competitions as a competitive programmer and as a coach.

name: Md. Akhtaruzaman Adnan
biography:
Md Akhtaruzzaman Adnan is a Senior Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, where he makes significant contributions to teaching and research. With over a decade of academic experience, he previously served as a faculty member at the University of Asia Pacific for 11 years, nurturing the next generation of computer scientists and engineers.

Adnan holds a Master’s degree in Computer Science from Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), one of Southeast Asia’s leading institutions, and a Bachelor’s degree from the Islamic University of Technology (IUT) in Gazipur. His academic background provides a strong foundation in both the theoretical and applied aspects of computer science.

His research interests encompass Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Cybersecurity in Healthcare settings, Natural Language Processing, and Algorithm Design. He has authored several publications in reputed journals and conferences. His scholarly contributions can be explored through his Google Scholar profile, which reflects his commitment to advancing knowledge in these fields.

Akhtaruzzaman’s dedication to academia is matched by his passion for mentoring students and fostering a vibrant research environment. His work aims to bridge the gap between academia and industry by promoting practical, impactful solutions to real-world problems. Beyond academia, he is actively involved in competitive programming and tech community development. He has played a pivotal role in organizing prestigious programming contests such as the International Collegiate Programming Contest (ICPC) and the National Collegiate Programming Contest (NCPC). Notably, he was one of the lead organizers of the ICPC World Finals 2022, hosted in Dhaka, Bangladesh, by UAP—a landmark event in the global programming community.

Additionally, Adnan is passionate about professional development. He regularly conducts advanced training courses for government and non-government professionals at institutions like NIMCA (National Institute of Mass Communication), BKICT (Bangladesh Korea Institute of Information and Communication Technology), and BCC (Bangladesh Computer Council). His dedication to education, research, and community engagement has established him as a respected figure in Bangladesh’s computer science landscape.

name: Mr. Moin Mostakim
biography:
I am Moin Mostakim, an individual driven by a deep passion for learning and building knowledge. Throughout my life, I have always been eager to stay up-to-date with cutting-edge technology and immerse myself in the world of research. My journey as a neural network architecture specialist and a blissful educator has been a fulfilling one, marked by continuous growth and exploration.

 

From an early age, I discovered my insatiable curiosity for understanding how things work. This innate curiosity led me to dive into the realm of technology, where I quickly became captivated by the intricate workings of neural networks. The ability of these systems to mimic the human brain and process complex information fascinated me beyond measure.

 

As I embarked on my educational journey, I dedicated myself to mastering the field of neural network architecture. I pursued a rigorous academic path, obtaining a degree in Computer Science and specialising in artificial intelligence. The more I delved into the subject, the more captivated I became by the immense potential of neural networks to revolutionise various domains, from healthcare to finance and beyond.

Driven by a relentless pursuit of knowledge, I embarked on numerous research projects, seeking to push the boundaries of what was possible in the field of neural networks. I embraced the challenges that arose, from designing innovative architectures to optimising performance and tackling complex problems. Through these research endeavours, I honed my skills and gained invaluable insights into the intricacies of neural network development.

 

 

Alongside my research endeavours, I found immense joy in sharing my knowledge with others. I discovered a passion for teaching and mentoring, realising the profound impact I could have on aspiring minds. Guiding students and fellow researchers in their exploration of neural networks brought me great satisfaction and allowed me to contribute to the growth of the field.

As a blissful educator, I foster an environment of curiosity, creativity, and critical thinking. I believe in empowering students to question the status quo, challenge existing paradigms, and push the boundaries of what is known. By instilling a deep understanding of neural network architecture and its applications, I aim to inspire the next generation of innovators and problem solvers.

 

My journey as Moin Mostakim has been one of continuous learning and growth. I remain passionate about staying at the forefront of technological advancements, embracing the ever-evolving landscape of neural network architecture. Through my research and educational endeavours, I aspire to contribute meaningfully to the field and make a lasting impact on the world.

name: Md. Saiful Islam
biography:
Hello! 

I am Saiful Islam, someone who is passionate about teaching and is currently enrolled as a Senior lecturer at BRAC University. As a dedicated mentor and educator, my teaching dynamics revolves around innovation, integrity, and inclusivity. My love for teaching has transcended classrooms and has been depicted on platforms like YouTube (https://www.youtube.com/@saifulcreates) which I created for the sole purpose of inspiring keen learners around the world. Through my tutorials and study materials, I  hope to live my dreams through my students and ensure education is accessible to all. 


I am wholeheartedly dedicated to nurture my students to achieve global milestones. My unwavering commitment behind the success of my students has earned me accolades like a Gold Medal at the International Science and Invention Fair organized by the Young Scientists Association. Besides, I have served as a Co-Principal Investigator at BRACU Mongol Tori and was selected to participate in NASA’s Mars Rover Competition that took place in Utah, USA. Additionally, I have served as the Co-Advisor of the Robotics Club. Together, all these experiences make me want to foster innovation and creativity amongst all students. 

At the same time, my leadership as a team leader of Techniche, a fully-funded Techno-Management Festival at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), showcases my organizational acumen and effective leadership qualities. This role contributed to securing a merit position at the prestigious Indian Rover Challenge (IRC), organized by Vellore Institute of Technology (VIT), Chennai, India. Apart from the above mentioned, my other contributions have been featured in several prominent news sources, for example - Mars Society South Asia, The Daily Star, BRACU Express.

name: Md. Tawhid Anwar
biography:
Md. Tawhid Anwar is currently working as a Senior Lecturer in the Computer Science and Engineering department of BRAC University. Prior to this, he served as an Undergraduate Teaching Assistant (aka Student Tutor) in the Department of CSE for almost 2 years. In addition, he was also a Student Mentor, where his responsibility was to guide fresher students in their academic improvement. Tawhid completed his M.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University with an outstanding academic record. He is a dedicated, hardworking, and decisive individual. He completed his B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University.

 

Tawhid oversees the BRAC University Entrepreneurship Development Forum - BUEDF club as the faculty co-advisor. He is well known for his leadership skills in managing a team noted for its high level of collaboration. He strives to contribute to the greater benefit of students through knowledge and wisdom. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence, Financial Market Analysis, and Machine Learning.

name: Sowmitra Das
biography:
I have had a very circuitous academic journey. I used to do a lot of Olympiads in high school. I attended the National Math Camp in 2012, and got hooked on math and problem-solving after that. These experiences have shaped a large part of my life and academic thinking (both positively and negatively). 

I did an undergraduate in EEE during which I developed an interest in Theoretical Physics. After graduating, I did an MSc in Theoretical Physics, and another MSc in Applied Mathematics. I have been teaching at the Dept. of CSE in BRAC University since 2019. My academic journey has encompassed all the fields of Math, Physics, CS and EEE. I like interdisciplinary study and research, although it gets overwhelming at times. 

My current research focus is on Quantum Computation and Quantum Information Theory. At BRAC, I co-taught Bangladesh’s first academic Quantum Computing course, and later introduced more advanced courses on this topic. My YouTube channel Rough Khata contains 500+ hours of recorded lectures on these topics. Previously, I have also worked on Computer Vision and Deep Learning projects, having been on the winning team of IEEE VIP Cup (2017). 

I play the Tabla and performed in many cultural events at BUET. I like listening to Classical Music. 

I have battled crippling depression and anxiety in my journey, and, after suffering for 6 years since 2012, I finally started therapy in 2018. One of my goals is to spread mental-health awareness among my students and close ones.

name: Najeefa Nikhat Choudhury
biography: I am Lecturer in the Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University and currently I am teaching Database Systems. I obtained my joint M.Sc. degree in ICT-Innovation from Aalto University, Finland and Technical University of Berlin, Germany in 2016. I received my B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University, Bangladesh in 2013. My Research interests include Natural Language Processing and Education Technology.  I was born in 1990 in a beautiful city called Sylhet, the largest hub of tea production estates in Bangladesh. Apart from being a full-time teacher, I love to travel, eat and occasionally read/write.

name: Ms. Tasnim Ferdous
biography:
Tasnim Ferdous is a Senior Lecturer at BRAC University. She earned her Masters in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Oregon State University (2016), receiving the Laurel Award for Outstanding Academic Performance, and her B.Sc. in Electrical and Electronic Engineering from AIUB (2012).

 

Her research combines biomedical signal processing, machine learning, and computational neuroscience. She focuses on non-invasive neurotechnology to improve cognitive performance and mental well-being. Her current work includes EEG-based neurofeedback for memory consolidation and automated sleep stage classification using adaptive deep learning models.

 

Tasnim previously worked as an Electrical Engineer at Electro Scientific Industries (ESI), USA) and as a faculty member at Northern University Bangladesh. She now mentors students and leads initiatives to advance neurofeedback, BCI, and sleep research at BRAC University.

 

Beside working, she loves to sketch and frame beautiful sights on paper. She also spends time reading on various topics, hiking within the woods and trying out different recipes.

name: Dewan Ziaul Karim
biography:
Dewan Ziaul Karim (DZK) is a Senior Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) at BRAC University, Dhaka, Bangladesh. He completed his M.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University, having initially started the program at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET). After completing his coursework at BUET, he transferred his credits to BRAC University to conduct his thesis and successfully earn his degree. He holds a B.Sc. in CSE from Ahsanullah University of Science and Technology (AUST).
 

Before joining BRAC University as a lecturer, Mr. Karim served as a full-time lecturer at Daffodil International University and as a part-time lecturer at Ahsanullah University of Science and Technology. He currently also serves as the coordinator of the ITEE training program at BRAC University, where he is responsible for scheduling training sessions and organizing seminars.

 

At BRAC University, he is currently teaching the Digital Logic Design theory and lab courses. Over the years, he has taught several undergraduate courses including Introduction to Computer Science, Data Communications, Database Management Systems, Software Engineering, and System Analysis and Design. He also regularly supervises final-year thesis and project work.

 

Mr. Karim’s primary research interests lie in computer vision and machine learning, with applications in medical imaging, agriculture, and social impact sectors. He is driven by the goal of advancing visual recognition systems through deep learning and solving real-world problems with innovative, practical solutions. His long-term vision is to contribute to research that meaningfully impacts society and inspires future generations of engineers.

Arif Shakil has joined BRAC University as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering as of May 2018. Before this, while he was still a student at BRAC University, he worked as a Student Tutor in the CSE Department of BRAC for almost 1.5 years(2016-2017 period), and worked as a Student's Mentor in BIL, BRAC University for a semester. Arif Shakil is currently pursuing his Masters degree in CSE from BRAC University and after completing his bachelors in CSE from BRAC University graduating in Fall 2017. Before then, he completed his O' Levels from BAF Shaheen English Medium School and then finished his A' Levels from British Council. Both the levels' curriculum was GCE of Edexcel Board.

biography: Mr. Syed Zamil Hasan Shoumo started his career at BRAC University on 2019 as a Senior Lecturer of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) under the School of Data and Sciences (SDS). He is currently pursuing his M.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from Brac University. His main area of research and interest is Artificial Intelligence (AI). On another note, he is an avid football fan and adores Chelsea Football Club. Lastly what he strives towards in life is to employ AI to make the world a bit safer, a bit quieter and a bit brighter.

name: Md. Saiful Bari Siddiqui
biography:
Md. Saiful Bari Siddiqui (Iftu) is a passionate educator and researcher with a strong background in engineering and computer science. His personality type is ENTP-A. His constant tendency to question the establishment and norms along with his expressive, headstrong, and independent nature characterize him as a person.
 

Saiful holds a B.Sc in Electrical and Electronic Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET), and an M.Sc in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University. During his undergraduate studies, he actively participated in extracurricular activities, including debating and leadership roles. He acted as the Vice President of the BUET Debating Club and was a member of BUETDC-Nandonik from 2018 to 2020, winning two national championships. He also has a keen interest in logical reasoning and problem-solving, and he has excelled in various academic pursuits, particularly olympiads. He has been a National and Divisional winner in the Bangladesh Mathematical Olympiad and Bangladesh Physics Olympiad. During the lockdown in 2020, he got familiar with the field of Data Science and Machine Learning through online platforms and self-study. This sparked the beginning of his research career in Applied Machine Learning and Deep Learning. Bioinformatics, Digital Signal Processing, Image Processing, and other facets of Computer Vision are also among his research interests. Currently, he is involved in multiple projects focusing on Medical Imaging and Bioinformatics.

In August of 2021, he began teaching at BRAC University. Since joining Brac University, he has taught a variety of courses including Image Processing (CSE428), and coordinated the CSE250 Lab (Circuits & Electronics). He is also a passionate sports enthusiast and a die-hard admirer of both Real Madrid and Bangladesh Cricket. The exciting areas of Bioinformatics and Sports Analytics are where he hopes to use his expertise in the future.

name: Mr. Md. Aquib Azmain
biography:
Md. Aquib Azmain is a Senior Lecturer at the Computer Science and Engineering Department, BRAC University. He has completed his BSc and MSc in Software Engineering from Institute of Information Technology, University of Dhaka. Mr. Aquib’s research interests are mainly focused on Automatic Program Repair, Data Mining and Deep Learning. He has several publications in reputed conferences.

In addition, Mr. Aquib was awarded ICT Ministry Fellowship for the purpose of his master's thesis. Also, he has won several awards on project showcasing contests organized by different universities.

name: Md. Sabbir Ahmed
biography:
Md. Sabbir Ahmed is a Senior Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Dhaka, Bangladesh. His academic work spans Data Science, Machine Learning, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI), Computer Vision, and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), with a focus on developing trustworthy, interpretable, and human-centered intelligent systems. He has contributed to multiple peer-reviewed international publications and actively collaborates on interdisciplinary research initiatives.
 

In addition to his research and teaching, he is actively engaged in academic leadership, research community building, and student mentorship. He contributes to undergraduate thesis supervision, faculty and student engagement initiatives, and the coordination of academic and professional programs involving students, faculty members, and external partners. Through his work, he is committed to strengthening research culture and fostering an inclusive, forward-looking academic environment.

biography:
Samiur Rahman has been actively engaged in academia in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University since May 2024. During his undergraduate studies, Samiur consistently demonstrated academic excellence. His research interests focus on Artificial Intelligence, Deep Learning, Data Science, and Machine Learning. With a strong foundation in both teaching and research, Samiur is dedicated to contributing meaningfully to the field of computer science.

name: Bijoy Ahmed Saiem
biography:
Bijoy Ahmed Saiem recently completed his BSc in Computer Science and Engineering from the Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) and is currently pursuing his MSc at the same university. He is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. Previously, he worked as a Research Assistant at BUET, a Lecturer at United International University, and a Software Engineer at Priyo. He brings a strong blend of academic and industry experience, with expertise spanning teaching, research, and software development.

His research interests include LLM/VLLM privacy and security, Natural Language Processing, and Human-Computer Interaction. Beyond academia, he gained valuable industry experience as a Software Engineer at Priyo. He also contributed to large-scale projects, such as Dataghurhi, funded by the World Bank, RIC, and EDGE, and the Warpo Dashboard, funded by WARPO. His work reflects a strong intersection of research innovation, technical development, and real-world application.

name: Souvik Ghosh
biography:
I am currently serving as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. I completed my undergraduate studies in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET), where I cultivated a strong interest in computational biology and machine learning.

My undergraduate thesis focused on bioinformatics, particularly Post translational modification site detection. I enjoy working at the intersection of biology and computer science, and my current research interests include protein language models, generative modeling for peptide design, and interpretable machine learning for biomedical applications.

Beyond academics, I find joy in teaching and mentoring, and I am passionate about making technical concepts accessible to students from diverse backgrounds. Outside the classroom or lab, I often spend my time reading literature, watching films or anime, or playing story games.

Md. Asif Haider served as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University from 2024-25. He earned his BSc. Engg. degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) in 2024. During his undergraduate studies, Asif completed two short, hybrid internships at OpenRefactory Bangladesh Limited and North West Power Generation Company Limited, where he focused on Secure Software Engineering and Machine Learning tasks, respectively. Additionally, he had actively mentored high school students for the past few years.

 

His research interests span Software Engineering, Large Language Models, and Human Factors in Computing and AI. He is also passionate about Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing techniques applied to the Healthcare domain, as well as contemporary topics in computational social science.
 

Asif is deeply thankful for the mentors and resources he has been blessed with throughout his journey. Driven by a strong enthusiasm for research and development, he is committed to contributing to the academic community with what students need most: quality education, right guidance, and motivating support. He is also an advocate of academic and cultural leadership activities on campus and beyond. When he is not busy with academic/research work, he is likely to be found exploring music, literature, and theaters.

 

Starting in fall 2025, Asif continues to work as a Graduate Teaching Assistant at the University of California, Irvine while on study leave to pursue his PhD.

name: Soumya Swagata Biswas
biography:
Soumya Swagata Biswas is a full-time Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. He earned his Bachelor's degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) in March 2025. Prior to joining BRAC University, he served as a full-time Lecturer at United International University (UIU).


 

Mr. Biswas is deeply passionate about both software development and academic research. His undergraduate thesis focused on enhancing defense mechanisms in Vertical Federated Learning Systems through the incorporation of Gradient Sparsification. His current research interests include developing adversarial attack strategies for Horizontal Federated Learning Systems.


 

Beyond his academic and professional commitments, he is an enthusiastic learner with a keen interest in astronomy. In his leisure time, he enjoys watching anime and binge-watching series. He actively engages in research discussions and is known for his skills as an impromptu speaker. He is also deeply committed to teaching and inspiring others.

name: Anika Priodorshinee Mrittika
biography:
Anika Priodorshinee Mrittika has been working as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering of the School of Data and Sciences at BRAC University since May 2024. She earned her Bachelor’s degree from the same department with the highest distinction. Prior to her current role, she spent nearly two years as Student Tutor and Adjunct Lecturer, combined, in the CSE department of BRAC University. 

 

At BRAC University, Ms. Mrittika currently teaches Numerical Methods theory and lab, Digital Logic Design theory, and the Human-Computer Interface lab. Previously, she also taught the Introduction to Robotics lab. Her research primarily focuses on Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) and Information and Communication Technologies for Development (ICT4D), and she has contributed to several research papers in these fields. She is deeply motivated by a strong desire to make a meaningful impact through education, aiming to create an environment that fosters continuous learning and personal growth.

name: Faria Binta Awal
biography:
Faria Binta Awal is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University and an Adjunct Lecturer at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET). She earned her B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from BUET in March 2025, where she graduated with distinction, ranking among the top 10 students in her cohort.

Her research focuses on developing computational methods for life science, spatial biology, and biomedical data science. She has worked on projects spanning machine learning for spatial transcriptomics, graph-based biological modeling, and deep learning for biomedical imaging. In undergraduate thesis, Faria developed SuNSTAMP, a supervised factorization framework integrating spatial transcriptomics with complementary molecular readouts, and contributed to the Spatialomics-Toolkit, presented at the Festival of Genomics & Biodata Boston 2025 workshop.

She has received multiple academic and research awards, including the RISE (Research and Innovation Centre for Science and Engineering) Research Grant and national recognition in programming contests such as the National Girls’ Programming Contest (Champion, 2022). Her team (BUET_Harmonica) ranked 23rd in ICPC Asia Dhaka Regional Contest 2024. Her ongoing work explores graph-based contrastive learning and computational models of cell–cell communication in regenerative biology.
publications: Supervised Factorization to Associate Spatial Transcriptomics with Complementary Molecular Readouts
Spatial Transcriptomics Data Analysis Workflow

I worked with Dr. Md. Abul Hassan Samee on the literature review and the design of a pipeline that ultimately led to the development of the Spatialomics-Toolkit,  which was later utilized during live demonstrations at a Festival of Genomics & Biodata Boston 2025 workshop.

name: Ariyan Hossain
biography:
Ariyan Hossain is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering from Brac University, graduating with the highest distinction. 

 

In his role as a Lecturer, Ariyan teaches core courses such as Data Structures (CSE220), Algorithms (CSE221) and an elective course Natural Language Processing (CSE440), actively engaging with students through both theory and lab sessions.

 

Ariyan also served as a Graduate Research Assistant in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. Under the supervision of Dr. Muhammad Nur Yanhaona, he worked on classifying news article framing using machine learning and natural language processing techniques. 

 

His research interests include machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, and AI ethics. His undergraduate thesis, supervised by Dr. Farig Yousuf Sadeque, focused on exploring and mitigating gender bias in word embeddings from transformer-based language models, with the findings submitted for publication. Ariyan continues to contribute to academic research while fostering a passion for teaching and mentorship.

name: Md. Fakhruddin Gazzali
biography:
MD. Fakhruddin Gazzali is a respected lecturer in Computer Science and Engineering Dept. at BRAC University. He earned his Bachelor of Science degree (BSc.) in the same field from the Islamic University of Technology (IUT), demonstrating his commitment to his discipline from the outset of his career.

 

At BRAC University, Mr. Gazzali is currently teaching courses in Algorithms, Data Communication, and Object Oriented Programming. These subjects are fundamental to the field of Computer Science and Engineering, and his expertise in these areas greatly benefits his students. Before his current role at BRAC, Mr. Gazzali served as a lecturer at United International University (UIU), where he taught a range of these subjects including Structured Programming Language. His teaching prowess was particularly evident in his DBMS and Algorithm course, where all the teams he guided became champions or achieved other top honors, reflecting his effective mentorship and the quality of his instruction.

 

In addition to his academic roles, Mr. Gazzali has also gained practical industry experience as a Software Developer Intern at Nilavo Technologies. This experience has enriched his teaching and research, providing him with valuable insights into the practical applications of computer science. Beyond his professional responsibilities, Mr. Gazzali is an active volunteer with the Mozilla Bangladesh Organization and the Firefox Social Support Team, demonstrating his commitment to the broader tech community.

 

Mr. Gazzali's research interests span Software Engineering, Computer Vision, and Computer Networks. His work in these areas is driven by his passion for exploring new frontiers in technology. Further attesting to his expertise, Mr. Gazzali holds a CCNA certificate, a globally recognized IT certification. His diverse interests and accomplishments reflect his dedication to continuous learning and contribution to the field of computer science.

name: Ipshita Bonhi Upoma
biography:
I am an aspiring physicist with an MSc in Particles, Strings and Cosmology from Durham University. My MSc. was funded by the Hatfield Lioness Scholarship which I was awarded from a pool of around 500 applicants in 2020.
 

After completion of my MSc. I resumed my role as a lecturer at the Computer Science and Engineering Department at Brac University in Bangladesh. I have instructed courses on Artificial Intelligence, Simulation and Modeling and Statistics. Currently, I am also conducting a course on Probabilistic Models. 
 

Majoring both in Physics and Computer Science in my bachelors (BRAC University), I am also enthusiastic about data science, AI and simulation. Especially the applications of these fields in modern day physics and solving social and practical problems intrigue me. Recently I have supervised thesis projects on Application of Physics Informed Neural Networks in Cosmological Parameter Predictions and, Use of Quantum Aware Algorithms in Image Protection in Generative AI. 
 

I am passionate about equity in STEAM and want to contribute to building a community of students studying fundamental sciences.  I have initiated and coordinated the participation of my home country, Bangladesh in the European Girls’ Mathematical Olympiad and have been the team leader/deputy leader in 2024, 2022 and 2021. In 2023, I started Bangladesh Girls' Mathematics Foundation aiming to empower girls of Bangladesh in Mathematics and STEM fields.  


When I am not working, I seek solace in poetry, stories, music and photography. I try to involve myself in different creative activities. I have been the diversity monitor at the Hatfield College's 175th Anniversary Play, Lions in the Bailey. In the 2022/23 academic session, I was elected to serve as the MCR Chair at Hatfield College, Durham University, where I represented and led the postgraduate student body.

 
I published a book of poetry at the age of 16 and still continue to write poetry in Bangla.
researchInterests: Quantum-Aware Image Encoding and Adversarial Perturbation
Physics-Informed Variational Autoencoders for Cosmological Field Reconstruction and Parameter Inference.

name: Utsha Kumar Roy
biography:
My name is Utsha Kumar Roy, and I am a robotics researcher and teacher with a strong interest in learning-based robot control, autonomous navigation, robot kinematics, and optimization. I am currently a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, where I teach undergraduate robotics courses, design hands-on laboratory sessions, and supervise student projects, while actively pursuing research on robot control, learning-enabled robotic systems, and end-to-end robotic process design, spanning modeling, perception, learning, and control.

I completed both my M.Sc. and B.Sc. degrees in Robotics and Mechatronics Engineering from the University of Dhaka, ranking first in both programs. My master’s research focused on human-aware robot navigation in dynamic crowds using reinforcement learning, where I developed principled state representations, reward formulations, and evaluation protocols for socially compliant navigation. During my undergraduate studies, I worked on 3D mapping for autonomous navigation using graph neural networks, which further strengthened my foundation in perception-driven robotic systems.

My research interests span robot kinematics and task-space control, reinforcement learning, optimization-based control methods, model predictive control, and GPU-accelerated simulation. Across multiple funded research projects in medical robotics, autonomous navigation, and underwater robotic systems, I have worked on integrating perception, learning, and control into complete end-to-end robotic pipelines. Alongside academia, I also bring industry experience, having developed ROS2-based simulation pipelines and control frameworks for industrial manipulation and autonomous systems. My current research efforts are directed toward publication in top-tier robotics conferences and journals, including IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters (RA-L) and Robotics: Science and Systems (RSS).

From a research philosophy perspective, I strongly believe in quality over quantity. Rather than pursuing a large number of loosely connected projects, I prefer to focus on a small set of well-defined problems, following a disciplined timetable and a structured research process. My aim is to construct work that is technically rigorous, reproducible, and capable of making a lasting impact, rather than producing rapid but shallow outputs.

Outside of research, I enjoy indoor games such as chess and table tennis. I am also keen on traveling, as it offers opportunities to experience diverse environments and ideas beyond the laboratory. In my free time, I enjoy playing the ukulele as a way to maintain creativity, balance, and mindfulness alongside academic work.

Khondoker Nazia Iqbal is currently working as a Lecturer in the Computer Science and Engineering Department of BRAC University. Her dedication to academic management has been recognized with a certification in course coordination. She actively collaborates with faculty members to enhance course structures, streamline assessments, and improve the overall learning experience for students.

Nazia’s passion for research lies in exploring advancements in Machine learning, Computer Vision, and Medical Image Processing. Through her publications in national and international conferences, she actively contributes to the research community. With a strong background in teaching and research, Khondoker Nazia Iqbal is dedicated to academic excellence, mentoring students, and expanding knowledge in computer science.

name: NIRJHOR DATTA
biography:
Nirjhor Datta is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Dhaka, Bangladesh. He received his Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University in 2024. Before joining as a full-time Lecturer in Summer 2025, he served as an Adjunct Lecturer for one year, where he contributed to both undergraduate teaching and academic mentoring.

His academic and research interests lie at the intersection of Natural Language Processing (NLP) for genomic and biomedical data, Medical Imaging, and Bioinformatics. He is particularly focused on developing computational models that can bridge the gap between biomedical sciences and modern AI, including applying transformer-based language models to genomic sequences and leveraging deep learning for improved medical image analysis.

name: Niloy Farhan
biography:
Niloy Farhan is currently serving as a Lecturer at BRAC University in Dhaka, Bangladesh. He is currently taking CSE221: Algorithms and CSE330: Numerical Methods courses, enriching their understanding through theory and lab classes. Before joining BRAC University, Niloy was working as Adjunct Lecturer at Dhaka International University. Niloy's journey in academia began as a Student Tutor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University from Fall 2022 to Summer 2023.

 

Niloy Farhan earned his Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from BRAC University, graduating with a remarkable CGPA of 3.94 out of 4.00 in September 2023. Prior to this, he completed his Higher Secondary Certificate from Notre Dame College and his Secondary School Certificate from Motijheel Govt. Boys’ High School, both with outstanding achievements.

 

Dedicated to academic research, Niloy has made significant contributions in the Natural Language Processing (NLP) field. He conducted “Research on the Latest Trends in Bangla Named Entity Recognition” as part of his Bachelor's Thesis at BRAC University, achieving an exceptional grade of A+. Additionally, he has published a paper titled "Ensemble of Gated Recurrent Unit and Convolutional Neural Network for Sarcasm Detection in Bangla," which was presented at the 2023 IEEE 13th Annual Computing and Communication Workshop and Conference. His current research interests include Applied Natural Language Processing, Machine Learning and Deep Learning.

name: Mr. Purbayan Das
biography:
Purbayan Das is an enthusiastic educator and researcher who has been a lecturer at BRAC University's Department of CSE since January 2023. He graduated in 2021 with a Bachelor of Science in EEE from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology, where he is currently pursuing a Master of Science in the same discipline. His passion for electronics prompted him to do research in the field of nanoelectronics, namely nanophotonics. His research interests include nanophotonics, optoelectronics, and neuromorphic computing, as well as their applications in other fields. He loves to be actively engaged in activities linked to problem-solving and research, constantly stretching the limits of his knowledge and abilities. In addition, he is an avid sports fan who used to regularly compete in hall and departmental sports events during his undergraduate studies. As part of a recent CSE Outdoor Team Building and Workshop, he also participated in a friendly football tournament among coworkers, which his team won.

Purbayan has always had a passion for teaching, and he considers himself fortunate to be able to pursue this passion professionally. He is able to pique his audience's interest in pondering unfamiliar ideas and looking at familiar things in novel ways, that make him well suited for teaching. Driven by his enthusiasm for teaching, he has been regularly producing high quality instructional materials for both teachers and students since joining this university, demonstrating his competence, capacity, and commitment to this profession.

He has honed his leadership skills throughout his school, college, and professional life. As the coordinator for the Circuits and Electronics (CSE250) course, he was given the opportunity to modify the course syllabi to better meet the needs of students. He enjoys playing cricket, football, and badminton in his spare time, as well as traveling and watching movies. As a person, he is best described by intuition, compassion, prospectivity, generosity, and idealism.

name: Prithu Mahmud
biography:
Prithu Mahmud is currently working as a Lecturer at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE), Brac University. He has experience teaching both theoretical and laboratory courses on electronics, VLSI, and circuit design.

He is pursuing an M.Sc. from the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering (EEE) at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) as an electronics major. During this time, he has worked on several research collaborations on high-temperature circuit design and deep-learning-based nanoscale transistor modeling with experts in academia and industry.

He has completed his B.Sc. from the same department. For his undergraduate thesis research, he worked on a Photonic Topological Insulator-based Multi-Wavelength Optical Demultiplexer with Piezoelectric Switch-Off on a Lithium Niobate platform.

His research interests include Photonic Integrated Circuits, Photonic Computing, Photonic Crystals, Photonic Topological Insulators, Deep Learning, Analog Integrated Circuits, VLSI, and Nanoscale Transistor Modeling.

name: Redowanul Akbar
biography:
Redowanul Akbar is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Bangladesh. He completed his bachelor’s degree in computer science and engineering in 2023 and joined BRAC University as an adjunct faculty member in Spring 2024. Later, in Summer 2024, he was appointed as a full-time lecturer of BRAC University.

His research interests span human - computer interaction, reinforcement learning, large language models, and robotics, with a particular emphasis on human-centered and hardware-aware intelligent systems. He is especially interested in applying machine learning and reinforcement learning techniques to real-world problems involving decision-making, autonomy, and system optimization across both software and embedded platforms.

Alongside his research activities, Redowanul Akbar is actively involved in teaching and curriculum development. He has taught courses including CSE340: Computer Architecture, CSE360: Computer Interfacing, CSE461: Introduction to Robotics, and embedded systems fundamentals. He is known for integrating theoretical foundations with hands-on experimentation, encouraging students to bridge the gap between abstract concepts and practical implementation.

Beyond academia, he is keen on interdisciplinary innovation that connects technological research with societal needs. His long-term academic goal is to pursue advanced research and contribute to impactful teaching and scholarship in computer science and engineering.

name: Sadif Ahmed
biography:
Sadif Ahmed is currently serving as a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. He earned his Bachelor of Science (BSc. Engg.) degree in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) in 2025 He has served as a Research Assistant at BUET, where he worked on Software Engineering Automation and Web UI automation, contributing to projects that enhance the reliability and security of software systems through intelligent tooling.

During his undergraduate studies, Sadif also conducted impactful research on secret breach detection in source code and issue reports using Large Language Models. He has developed a wide range of practical tools and platforms in areas like blockchain, home automation, and anime data indexing.

His research interests span Software Engineering and Security, Large Language Models, and Developer Privacy. He is particularly passionate about using Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing techniques to advance software quality, reliability, and confidentiality.

Sadif is grateful for the academic mentors and collaborative opportunities that have supported his journey. Driven by a strong enthusiasm for both research and development, he aims to contribute meaningfully to the academic and software engineering communities. Outside of academics, he enjoys sports, gaming and watching anime and tv shows.
