name: Shomen Kundu
biography:
Shomen Kundu is working as a Lecturer in the Department of CSE, BRAC University. He has experience teaching both theoretical and laboratory courses on electronics, VLSI, and circuit design. Shomen has always had a passion for teaching, and he considers himself fortunate to be able to pursue this passion professionally. Since joining the university, he has consistently demonstrated his expertise, dedication, and passion for the profession by producing high-quality instructional materials for both educators and students. His contributions reflect his competence and commitment to enhancing the learning experience. He is currently serving as the coordinator for the VLSI Design (CSE460) course. 
 

He received his BSc. from the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering (EEE), BUET in 2024, with a major in Electronics and Photonics. Throughout his academic journey, he has gained research experience in various fields, including DFT Simulation, 2D materials, Quantum Devices, 2D DG-FETs, Nanoelectronics, Analog Circuit Design, and VLSI. He is also interested in different algorithms of Machine Learning and Deep Learning. Apart from studies, he has been the Assistant General Secretary of Environment Watch: BUET. His career ambition is to work as a researcher.

He has a friendly personality and tries to develop an interactive environment during teaching sessions. Besides, he likes traveling, cooking, and enjoys playing chess.

name: Asif Shahriar
biography:
Asif Shahriar is currently working as a Lecturer in the Computer Science and Engineering department of BRAC University. He completed his B.Sc. in Computer Science and Engineering from Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) in 2024, with a strong academic record. During his third year, Asif completed a hybrid internship at North West Power Generation Company Limited, where he helped develop a machine learning algorithm for predicting faulty ultrasonic flow-meters. His current research interests span across automated computer and network protocol security frameworks, natural language processing models, and Trustworthy LLMs. Alongside raw-performance improvement, he also values efficiency, modularity, and automation (what good are agents if not automated?).

 

Asif is grateful to all the mentors and supervisors who have guided and supported him throughout his academic and research journey. He believes in “persistence until improvement”, even if it requires going the extra mile – and hopes his students would, too.

name: K. M. Shadman Wadith
biography:
K.M. Shadman Wadith is a lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. Before this, he was a lecturer at the Green University of Bangladesh. He completed his B.Sc. from the Department of CSE, University of Dhaka. 
His research focus is Reinforcement Learning, particularly Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). He has dedicated his career to exploring scalable, safe, and adaptive solutions for autonomous systems. His research includes privacy-preserving, decentralized MARL algorithms, and his master's research investigates hybrid MARL approaches that bridge offline and online paradigms for adaptive decision-making in dynamic environments. Alongside his core research, his interests extend to trustworthy and robust AI and NLP, with specific challenges in creating a Bangla language model.

name: Mr. Shakir Rouf
biography:
Mr.Shakir Rouf completed his BSc. in Computer Science and Engineering in December 2019 from BRAC University. Afterwards, he immediately found a position at BRAC University as an Adjunct Lecturer in January 2020. He continued as an Adjunct Lecturer up until May 2024. He is now working as a full-time Lecturer at BRAC University as of May 2024. Before his academic career, he was a student right here at BRAC University. His experiences throughout the four years of his Undergrad inspired him to pursue a career in the academic profession. Apart from his job, he actively enjoys gaming and listening to music. While he doesn’t necessarily focus on any particular genre in either of his hobbies, he enjoys whatever feels good. “Any amount of money never bought a second of time” is one of his favorite quotes, and he passionately believes in getting things done on time, and giving time to the ones important.

His door is always open to any student looking for proper guidance in academia or life.

name: Sukarna Sarker
biography:
Sukarna Sarker is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. She joined the department as a full-time faculty member in Summer 2025, after serving as an adjunct lecturer since Fall 2023. She holds a Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University, graduating with the highest distinction. During her undergraduate studies, she was awarded a merit-based scholarship for her academic excellence. In addition, she successfully completed a faculty development training program under the Learning and Teaching Innovation Center (LTIC), further strengthening her pedagogical skills.

Prior to her faculty appointment, Ms. Sarker worked as a student tutor, providing academic support to students from both the School of Data and Sciences (MNS) and the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University. Her early engagement in teaching and mentoring roles reflects her commitment to academic development and student success.

She currently serves as the course coordinator for CSE420: Compiler Design, overseeing the course’s delivery and academic integrity across multiple sections.

Ms.  Sarker’s professional interests lie in computer science education, compiler design,machine learning and innovative teaching methodologies. She is dedicated to creating an engaging and inclusive learning environment that empowers students to reach their full potential.

name: Showvik Biswas
biography:
Showvik Biswas is currently serving as a lecturer at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, School of Data & Sciences, BRAC University. He recently completed his undergraduate degree from the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology. 

With an undergraduate thesis on bioinformatics, Showvik specializes in developing methods for dry lab experiments on cell tissue data. He is presently also exploring the intersection of the domains of natural language processing and computer security, which he has always had enthusiasm for.

When not preoccupied with academics, Showvik can be found playing video games, behind a drum kit practicing with his band, or amidst heated arguments about football, mainly the misfortunes of Manchester United.

biography:
Mohammad Tahsin Alam is currently serving as a full-time lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, a role he assumed in July 2024. 

 

He completed his B.Sc. degree from the Department of Electrical and Electronic Engineering (EEE), BUET in 2024, specializing in Electronics and Photonics. Tahsin’s research interests center on advanced fields such as Plasmonics and Opto-electronics, and he has conducted work in emerging areas like Perovskite solar cells and 2D materials. As an educator, Tahsin strives to create an engaging and interactive atmosphere for his students in the classroom.

 

In his free time, he enjoys watching anime and reading books.

name: Mr. Mirza Md. Tanjim Shorif Mugdho
biography:
Mirza MD. Tanjim Shorif Mugdho started as a Lecturer of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University in 2019. Prior to this, he joined as a trainer of Bangladesh National Math Team in 2013 following his retirement as a team member himself. He represented Bangladesh among 101 countries in 53rd International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO) -2012 held in Argentina. Among his wide array of interests, the childhood passion for music turned him into a singer by profession once he won the title of “Channel i Shera Kontho-2010”.

Mr. Mugdho completed his SSC from Mymensingh Zilla School and HSC from Notre Dame College, Dhaka with GPA 5.00/5.00 in all board exams. For Bachelor's degree, he studied at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET).

name: Towshik Anam Taj
biography:
Mr. Towshik Anam Taj completed both his undergraduate and postgraduate degree from BRAC University in Computer Science and Computer Science and Engineering respectively. He has been awarded the prestigious Vice-Chancellor’s Gold Medal recently for his performance in postgraduate studies. He has served the university as a student tutor which is equivalent to undergraduate teaching assistant during his undergraduate days and was awarded with certification by the Department of Computer Science and Engineering for his services. Mr. Towshik Anam Taj has also worked as a Junior SQA Automation Engineer in Automation Solutionz, a Canada based software firm who provides testing solutions for large scale software and mobile applications.

Mr. Towshik Anam Taj is currently serving as an adjunct lecturer in the same department and has experience on teaching a broader range of courses starting from Introduction to Computer Science, Digital Logic Design to teaching advanced level courses such as Artificial Intelligence, Computer Networks etc. His research interests include Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Cyber Security, Cryptography and other subdomains of the mentioned research areas. He has published research articles in the area of Artificial Intelligence and Cryptography under prestigious conferences and journals and he is currently working on numerous research projects which are exploring various research domains such as Computer Vision, Machine Learning associated Cyber Security etc.

name: Umme Jannat Taposhi
biography:
Umme Jannat Taposhi is a Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Bangladesh. She earned both her Bachelor’s degree (Fall 2023) and Master’s degree (Summer 2025) in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University, graduating with the Highest Distinction in both. 
 

Her research interests lie at the intersection of:

Human–Computer Interaction (HCI)
Social Computing and Digital Behavior
Digital Health
Usability and User Experience (UX) Analysis
Human–AI Collaboration and Explainable Systems
 
Taposhi’s work focuses on understanding how people interact with intelligent technologies across social, educational, and healthcare contexts. She is particularly interested in designing data-driven yet human-centered systems that improve usability, inclusivity, and trust. Through her teaching and research, she aims to bridge the gap between human behavior and computational intelligence to create more inclusive and impactful digital experiences.

name: Yasmin Nadia
biography:
Yasmin Nadia graduated from Brac University in 2023 with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering.

 

She began her professional journey as a Software Engineer Trainee at BJIT Ltd., where she worked with the MERN stack from August 2023 to November 2023.

 

From October 2023, she served as an Adjunct Lecturer, teaching Data Communication, Discrete Mathematics, Computer Networks Theories, Object-Oriented Programming, and Microprocessor Labs. In October 2025, she joined as a Lecturer, continuing to teach the same subjects.

 

Her research interests include deep learning, computer vision, multimodal learning, and conditional content generation, with applications in neurological disorder detection and healthcare AI.

name: Sumaiya
biography: Sumaiya is a lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Bangladesh. Her research interests span human-computer interaction, reinforcement learning, data science, and hardware-oriented domains such as human-robot interaction. She is particularly interested in interdisciplinary research that bridges real-world problems with technological solutions. She completed her bachelor’s degree in 2023 and joined BRAC University in 2024, where she has taught courses including Computer Architecture, Computer Interfacing, Microprocessors and Introduction to Robotics. Her current research focus includes usable security, information assurance, and human factors in computing systems.

name: Ayesha Siddika
biography:
Ayesha Siddika is a dedicated educator and computer science enthusiast, currently serving as an Adjunct Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University since January 2024. Her dedication to education is evident from her previous roles as a Student Tutor in both the Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) and Mathematics and Natural Sciences (MNS) departments at BRAC University from February 2021 to April 2022 and February 2020 to January 2021, respectively. Ayesha's commitment to academic excellence is underscored by her numerous accolades during her undergraduate years, where she graduated with the Highest Distinction in 2022 and received recognition on the Vice Chancellor's Honor List for outstanding results in seven semesters. She also achieved a coveted spot on the Dean's Honor List for exceptional performance in one semester, along with consistently earning Merit Scholarships based on her academic achievements in various semesters from Fall 2019 to Spring 2022.

Currently pursuing her Master's degree in Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Ayesha continues to excel in her academic pursuits. Her passion extends to the realms of Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and active research, reflecting her keen interest in staying at the forefront of technological advancements. Ayesha's dynamic engagement with emerging technologies positions her as a valuable contributor to both academia and the field of Computer Science.

Kaji Sajjad Hossain, an Adjunct Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University since October 2024, is a dedicated academic and researcher passionate about cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, and natural language processing. His research focuses on applying machine learning and deep learning techniques to enhance network security and intelligent systems.

Before joining as a faculty member, Sajjad gained industry experience as a Technology Intern at BRAC Bank PLC and served as a Student Tutor at BRAC University for core computer science courses. Sajjad’s teaching approach emphasizes simplifying complex technical concepts and encouraging students to apply knowledge through experimentation and problem-solving. Outside of academia, he enjoys exploring new technologies, playing badminton, and watching football matches.

name: Ayon Roy
biography:
Ayon Roy is an Adjunct Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Dhaka. He graduated with a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science and Engineering from BRAC University in February 2025, graduating with the highest distinction in his program.

 

Before joining BRAC University as an Adjunct Lecturer, Ayon held several technical roles. He completed a Digitalist Internship in the AI ​​and Automation Department at Banglalink Digital Communications Limited and worked as an Digital Finance and Services Intern with the ML Team at NCC Bank PLC. During his studentship, he also worked as a Undergraduate Student Tutor/ Teaching Assistant in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, supporting core academic subjects.

 

Ayon's undergraduate thesis focused on the advancement of Bangla machine translation using informal datasets. This work involved creating a state-of-the-art dataset and applying sequence-to-sequence models to improve translation quality for low-resource and informal regional languages. His current research interests include Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP), LLM, Cryptography.

name: Farah Binta Haque
biography:
Farah Binta Haque is currently serving as an Adjunct Lecturer in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at BRAC University, Bangladesh, since June 2025. Alongside her teaching role, she is also working as a Research Associate at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET) from January 2025. Previously, she contributed as a Lecturer at Manarat International University (MIU). Her academic journey in teaching began as an Undergraduate Student Tutor at the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, BRAC University, from Fall 2022 to Fall 2023.

 

Farah completed her Bachelor of Science in Computer Science at BRAC University, graduating with Highest Distinction. Throughout her undergraduate studies, she was consistently recognized on the Vice Chancellor’s Honor List and the Dean’s Honor List for her academic excellence. She also earned performance-based scholarships in every available semester for maintaining outstanding CGPA. Beyond university, her academic achievements were distinguished by multiple merit-based government scholarships, including the Talentpool Scholarships in PSC, JSC, and SSC, and HSC.


Her research focuses on Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Signal Processing, and Natural Language Processing. She has published in both journals and conferences, including a paper on Hybrid 3D CNN-LSTM for rs-fMRI-Based Parkinson’s Prediction in the Journal of Independent Studies and Research – Computing and a conference paper on Convex Hull Optimization for Mobile Finance Agent Allocation at the World Conference on Information Systems for Business Management.

name: Shafin Islam Ohin
biography:
Shafin Islam Ohin is an adjunct faculty member in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University. He completed his B.Sc. in Computer Science from Brac University in 2025 with the highest distinction. Throughout his undergraduate studies, he received a merit-based scholarship and was consistently placed on the Vice Chancellor’s and Dean’s lists. His undergraduate thesis focused on Spoken Question Answering in Bangla using Large Language Models, reflecting his interest in Natural Language Processing.

 

Before joining as a faculty member, he served as a Student Tutor in the same department. He currently teaches CSE110 (Programming Language I) Lab and CSE422 (Artificial Intelligence) Lab. Shafin enjoys solving problems and has participated in several onsite programming contests during his undergraduate years. He finds joy in teaching topics that fascinate him, sharing the sense of wonder he felt when learning something new.

name: Masuk Ridwan Saumo
biography:
Masuk Ridwan is a EEE graduate with research interests spanning quantum communication, astrophysics, and cosmology. His undergraduate thesis was based on quantum error-correcting codes using Qiskit. Moreover, his research experience includes gravitational lensing and galaxy modeling in collaboration with NASA’s Space Telescope Science Institute, 21 cm signal observation and simulation with Center for Astrophysics, Space Science and Astronomy (CASSA), IUB and CubeSat development with Bangladesh Space Research and Remote Sensing Organization (SPARRSO).

 

His current research focuses on early-universe cosmology, including stochastic inflation, and modeling primordial black hole formation based on a Summer Internship organized by International Centre for Theoretical Physics (ICTP).

 

Beyond research, he has been actively engaged in community service and science outreach, contributing to educational initiatives for underprivileged children and organizing academic and cultural events that promote scientific awareness.

name: Md Ishmam Tasin
biography:
Ishmam is currently a Graduate Research Assistant in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering at Brac University, where he also completed his undergraduate studies in Computer Science. His research focuses on the intersection of privacy, security, HCI, blockchain, artificial intelligence (AI), cloud computing, and augmented/virtual reality (AR/VR). A core emphasis of his work is understanding the human factors involved in interacting with these technologies, examining how people from diverse backgrounds engage with them, the different kinds of challenges that arise in these interactions, and identifying effective ways to address those challenges. He has worked on projects like SecureUS that combine a social computing style platform with security awareness goals. He has published multiple research papers, including works in IEEE TrustCom, ACM SAC, IEEE Access, ACM COMPASS, and CHIRA.

 

Broadly, his research aims to explore these key questions:

 

How can existing and emerging technologies (such as AI, blockchain, and cloud computing) be leveraged to solve pressing societal problems, particularly in underserved communities?
How do social, economic, cultural, and interpersonal factors influence the security and privacy, adoption, acceptance, usage patterns, and evolution of these technologies, and how do they inform technological design decisions?
In what ways do emerging technologies like AI shape various facets of human life, including societies, cultures, norms, and organizations, and how can we guide human-AI interactions toward positive outcomes for human well-being?
 

 

Research Interest: 
Human-computer interaction (HCI); information, communication technologies, and development (ICTD); interaction techniques for AR/VR and wearable devices; usable privacy and security; socio-technical systems and interdisciplinary technology adoption; blockchain and cloud computing for secure, scalable interactive systems.

name: Rawhatur Rabbi
biography:
Rawhatur Rabbi is currently pursuing his Master’s in Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) at BRAC University and serves as a Graduate Research Assistant in the CSE Department under the supervision of Dr. Aniqua Nusrat Zereen. His research interests focus on machine learning, particularly deep learning and computer vision.

Before his current role, Rawhatur was a Research Associate in the BBS Department of BRAC University, where he contributed to database and website creation and research forecasting. He also has experience as a Software Engineer trainee in AI and ML, working on Dr. Chashi, an in-house product of Medina Tech Ltd, which is dedicated to data-driven farming to optimize and automate crop disease detection.

During his undergraduate years, he worked as a Student Tutor in the MNS Department of BRAC University. He also gained valuable experience as a part-time QA and Automation Tester at MasterWizR, a Norway-based SaaS company. There, he developed and executed comprehensive test plans and cases for software applications to ensure they met system specifications. He also interned at Parallaxlogic Infotech early in his academic career.

Alongside his enthusiasm for research and academics, Rawhatur actively participated in extracurricular activities. He spent four years with the Robotics Club of BRAC University (ROBU), where he became the Director of the Marketing and Finance Department. During his time with ROBU, he led many sessions on the basics of robotics.

Before his undergraduate journey with BRACU, he completed his schooling at Monipur School and Notre Dame College.

name: Md Ashadullah-All-Galib
biography:
Md. Ashadullah-All-Galib
IT Professional | Lab Technical Officer at BRAC University

Md. Ashadullah-All-Galib is an IT professional with over 7 years of experience in the university IT sector. He has been working at BRAC University for the past 2 years and has recently been promoted to Assistant Manager (LTO-2) in recognition of his dedication and technical expertise.

 

In his role, he is responsible for managing and maintaining computer labs, preparing software environments and system configurations for each semester, and providing technical support to faculty and students. He also oversees IT equipment such as computers, peripherals, projectors, and printers, ensuring smooth and uninterrupted operations.

 

Alongside maintenance duties, he plays an important role in ensuring lab security, monitoring network and system usage, and protecting IT infrastructure from security threats like malware and spyware.

 

Committed to continuous improvement, he is always eager to learn, upgrade his skills, and contribute to enhancing the university’s IT ecosystem.
